result:
The original assertion is fixed — we've moved past it to a different runtime error. The build is green.

**Root cause:** Three error-formatting sites in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs` were left as placeholders that omitted the label/variable name from the error message:
- `undef_goto` (line 2741) emitted `"no visible label for <goto> at line N"` instead of `"no visible label 'name' for <goto>..."`.
- `jumpscopeerror` (line 2565) omitted both goto name and variable name.
- `checkrepeated` (line 3702) emitted just `"label already defined"` with no name or line.

The test `errmsg` helper at line 16-18 of `goto.lua` asserts `string.find(msg, "label 'l1'")` etc., so the missing names made every `errmsg` call fail.

**Fix:** Pulled the bytes out of `gt.name`/`vd.name` via `as_bytes()` + `String::from_utf8_lossy` and formatted them into the C-Lua–shaped messages (`"no visible label '%s' for <goto> at line %d"`, `"<goto %s> at line %d jumps into the scope of local '%s'"`, `"label '%s' already defined on line %d"`).

**New state:** `errmsg(...)` asserts no longer fire; the test now reaches a later failure — `attempt to perform arithmetic on a nil value` — which is a distinct bug for the mega-loop's next pass. `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green; no temporary instrumentation was added.
